hypothesis: Revert REMATCH_THRESHOLD 0.20 → 0.30 under gallery-130 identification. Run_017 at REMATCH=0.20 produced 46/130 (35 %) enrollment dedup collisions on SCface — distinct subjects merging into existing identities at first-contact. Expected at REMATCH=0.30: dedup collisions drop to single digits; rank1_gk on surviving enrollees remains ≥ 90 % on d1_far (per run_015’s verification numbers the embedder is strong at this threshold). If dedup stays high, collisions are embedder-bound (frontal mugshot cosines > 0.30 for many cross-subject pairs) and REMATCH is not the right lever.

scenarios.notes: gallery=90 · rank1_gk=45/87 (0.52) · rank1_emb=84/87 (0.97) · rank5_emb=86/87 (0.99) · enroll: 39 embed-fail, 1 dedup-collision · 40 queries skipped (subject not in gallery)
gallery=90 · rank1_gk=90/90 (1.00) · rank1_emb=90/90 (1.00) · rank5_emb=90/90 (1.00) · enroll: 39 embed-fail, 1 dedup-collision · 40 queries skipped (subject not in gallery)
gallery=90 · rank1_gk=89/90 (0.99) · rank1_emb=90/90 (1.00) · rank5_emb=90/90 (1.00) · enroll: 39 embed-fail, 1 dedup-collision · 40 queries skipped (subject not in gallery)

notes: Revert. Per the run’s decision rule d1_far rank1_gk must drop ≤ 5 pts; it dropped 42 pts (94 % → 52 %). Hypothesis half-confirmed: dedup collisions DID drop dramatically (46 → 1), so REMATCH=0.20 is clearly mis-calibrated for multi-identity enrollment. BUT the 39 newly-UNCERTAIN enrollments (subjects whose frontal mugshot cosines land in [0.20, 0.30) against someone already in the bank) now fail to enroll at all, and tail-similarity d1_far query matches in the same cosine band now fall below REMATCH and get rejected — the exact failure mode run_016 was solving. Deeper finding: REMATCH_THRESHOLD at gatekeeper.py:161 conflates two decisions with different optimal values: (a) cross-track dedup at first-contact (“is this a duplicate of someone enrolled?” → wants strict, ~MATCH_THRESHOLD=0.40); (b) re-acquisition of a lost track (“did this new track_id return the same person?” → wants loose, ~0.20). One knob can’t satisfy both. Architectural fix (run_019): route the first-contact dedup check through MATCH_THRESHOLD while leaving REMATCH_THRESHOLD governing the same-identity-returning path. S1 floor 100 %. Collision count, enrollment count, and rank-k numbers are now documented per-REMATCH; run_019 compares against the run_017 baseline for dedup and run_016 for d1_far retrieval.
